Apple___Black_rot.cause: Fungal infection caused by Botryosphaeria obtusa, usually developing in warm and humid conditions.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Apply fungicides like captan or myclobutanil and remove infected leaves or fruits.
Apple___Black_rot.organic_solution: Spray neem oil or baking soda solution weekly to control fungal growth.

Apple___healthy.cause: No disease detected. The plant appears healthy.
Apple___healthy.treatment: No treatment required. Maintain proper watering and nutrient supply.
Apple___healthy.organic_solution: Use compost and organic fertilizers to keep the plant healthy.

Potato___Early_blight.cause: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani, often appearing in warm and humid environments.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Use fungicides such as chlorothalonil or mancozeb and remove infected leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.organic_solution: Apply neem oil spray and ensure proper air circulation around plants.

Potato___Late_blight.cause: Caused by the pathogen Phytophthora infestans which spreads rapidly in cool and wet conditions.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides like copper fungicide or chlorothalonil and remove infected plants.
Potato___Late_blight.organic_solution: Use copper-based organic sprays and avoid overhead watering.

Potato___healthy.cause: No disease detected in the potato plant.
Potato___healthy.treatment: No treatment needed. Maintain good soil nutrients and irrigation.
Potato___healthy.organic_solution: Use organic compost and mulch to keep soil healthy.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.cause: Fungal infection caused by Passalora fulva which thrives in high humidity.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve ventilation and apply fungicides like chlorothalonil.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.organic_solution: Spray neem oil or potassium bicarbonate solution weekly.

Tomato___Late_blight.cause: Disease caused by Phytophthora infestans which spreads quickly in wet conditions.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply copper fungicides and remove infected plant parts.
Tomato___Late_blight.organic_solution: Use copper-based organic fungicides and maintain good airflow.

Tomato___Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.cause: Virus transmitted by whiteflies which causes yellowing and curling of leaves.
Tomato___Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: Control whiteflies using insecticides and remove infected plants.
Tomato___Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.organic_solution: Use neem oil spray and introduce natural predators like ladybugs.

Tomato___healthy.cause: No disease detected. The tomato plant is healthy.
Tomato___healthy.treatment: No treatment required. Continue regular plant care.
Tomato___healthy.organic_solution: Use organic fertilizers and maintain balanced watering.
